task.title: Persist metadata-only tool invocation outcomes
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/544 by wrapping the existing permission protocol with durable intent and terminal evidence while retaining only non-sensitive metadata.
acceptance_criteria: A unique invocation stream records intent before authorization and exactly one denied, succeeded, or failed terminal outcome afterward.
Replay fails closed on malformed or impossible histories and duplicate IDs fail before authorization or execution.
Exact input, output, errors, policy details, and credentials are never persisted.
Terminal append failure returns the exact in-memory result and leaves an ambiguous pending stream without retry.

attempts.summary: Fixed retention and crash semantics before implementation.
attempts.diagnostic: Persisting full inputs or outputs would create a sensitive unbounded retention surface, while retrying an intent-only stream could duplicate an external effect.
attempts.patch: Issue 544 constrained evidence to invocation and tool identities, declared effect, and terminal status; pending streams remain ambiguous and are never automatically resumed.

attempts.summary: Established RED with durable invocation integration tests.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused target failed on missing durable store, invocation identity, projection, status, wrapper, and error APIs.
attempts.patch: Retained coverage for IDs, event ordering, denial, success, adapter failure, duplicate protection, malformed replay, source chains, and terminal persistence failure.

attempts.summary: Implemented metadata-only event streams around the existing protocol.
attempts.diagnostic: Intent commits before authorization; invoke_tool remains the only permission and adapter path; a metadata-only terminal event follows the exact in-memory result. Replay accepts only one intent and at most one terminal event.
attempts.patch: Added ToolInvocationStore, invocation projections and statuses, versioned events, durable error structure, and invoke_tool_durable without changing the original non-durable API.

attempts.summary: Hardened ambiguous-pending verification during independent review.
attempts.diagnostic: Review identified that the terminal-append failure fixture should prove the durable intent prefix remains visible instead of only matching an error variant.
attempts.patch: Strengthened the fixture to reopen and assert the pending projection while preserving the exact in-memory result in TerminalPersistence. Nine focused tests and pinned verification then passed.

attempts.summary: Passed the hosted Rust gate and merged after review automation was limited.
attempts.diagnostic: Rust quality gate run 30035099600 succeeded. CodeRabbit reported an adaptive review limit rather than producing line review; the independently reviewed, locally verified one-commit slice still satisfied repository merge requirements.
attempts.patch: Pull request 545 squash-merged as 679d906037f7281022f6161af3e16bd1c584dc2a and closed issue 544.

outcome.summary: Vela can durably audit metadata-only tool intent and terminal state without retaining exact payloads or retrying ambiguous external work.

verification.command: nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test durable_tool_invocations # 9 passed

lessons: Durable intent must precede authorization so every attempted effect has an audit prefix.
Metadata-only evidence minimizes retention risk while exact results remain available to the immediate caller.
An intent-only stream is ambiguous after a crash and must not trigger automatic retry.
Terminal persistence errors should preserve both the durable ambiguity and the exact in-memory result.
